name: quota
description: /quota <args> — Claude account 5h/7d usage limits + multi-account registry + live credential swap + per-account nicknames. Verbs: list (= status = bare · unified all-accounts table — active account ★ + live 5h/7d, every other account its last per-account cache, `—` = never fetched) · all (same table, every account fetched LIVE in one shot, fetched in PARALLEL via _parallel_refresh) · NOTE: the bare/list view renders from cache instantly then spawns a DETACHED background refresh (stale-while-revalidate, parallel) of stale non-active accounts (cache > 300s, with backup creds) so the NEXT view trends fresh without `all` — the view never blocks on the network; a dead/expired backup token is skipped for a 1800s cooldown (shows `—` until `refresh <ref>` renews it); the active account keeps a 45s inline live-refresh · add [<nick>] (snapshot account + capture creds; optional nickname) · nick <ref> [<nick>] (set/clear nickname; aliases name · rename) · switch <ref> (live cred swap, verified + rollback) · remove <ref> · refresh [<ref>] (re-fetch usage into per-account cache; renews expired tokens) · autoadd (silent SessionStart-hook path — registers the active account iff not yet tracked; driven by the quota-autoadd hook plugin) · help. <ref> = nickname (e.g. claude1) · email · numeric index — pick any. 45s per-account cache (429 throttle). Per-account OAuth blob backup in macOS keychain svc 'sidecar-quota' / linux ~/.sidecar/quota/creds/<email>.json (0600). own-accounts-serial only (ToS — never multi-tenant). ⚠ depends on Anthropic's non-public oauth/usage + oauth/token endpoints (ccmon idiom) — may break if rotated.
